name: ARTERIOVENOUS MALFORMATION
definition: congenital abnormality consisting of abnormal dilated closely packed pathologic vessels → diffuse nidus type AVM CAMS = WYBURN-MASON SYNDROME = BONNET-DECHAUME-BLANC DISEASE

demographics.Histo: affected arteries have thin walls (no elastica, small amount of muscularis)
demographics.Prevalence: 0.02-0.15% for sporadic AVM; 2% for syndromic AVM (hereditary
demographics.Peak age: 20-40 years; 80% by end of 4th decade; 20% < 20 years of age; M=F

dominant_finding: tangle of intensely enhancing tubular structures = nidus ← tortuous dilated vessels (in
discriminating_features: NO mass effect (due to replacement of normal brain tissue) unless complicated by
adjacent parenchymal atrophy ← vascular steal + ischemia
thinning / thickening of skull at contact area with AVM

NECT: irregular lesion with large feeding arteries + draining veins
mixed density (60%): dense large vessels + hemorrhage + calcifications
isodense lesion (15%): recognizable by mass effect
low density (15%): brain atrophy due to ischemia

3-D TOF demonstrates feeding arteries + nidus + draining veins
normal brain parenchyma interspersed between abnormal vessels
ANGIO: grossly dilated efferent + afferent vessels with a racemose tangle ("bag of worms")
arteriovenous shunting into at least one early draining vein
negative angiogram ← compression by hematoma / thrombosis
extensive transdural supply via middle meningeal artery

clinical.Cx: (1)   Hemorrhage (common): bleeding on venous side ← increased pressure / ruptured
